Morbid (severe) obesity due to excess calories
Other obesity due to excess calories
Body mass index [BMI] 70 or greater, adult
Fatty (change of) liver, not elsewhere classified
Acute and subacute hepatic failure without coma
Urinary tract infection, site not specified
Nephrotic syndrome with unspecified morphologic changes
Nephropathy induced by other drugs, medicaments and biological substances
Sepsis due to Methicillin susceptible Staphylococcus aureus
Sepsis due to Methicillin resistant Staphylococcus aureus
Unspecified Escherichia coli as cause of diseases classified elsewhere
Enterocolitis due to Clostridioides difficile, not specified as recurrent
Infection following a procedure, other surgical site, initial encounter
Delirium due to known physiological condition
Epilepsy, unspecified, not intractable, without status epilepticus
Anoxic brain damage, not elsewhere classified
Major depressive disorder, single episode, unspecified
Age-related osteoporosis without current pathological fracture
Infection following a procedure, initial encounter
Disruption of external operation wound, not elsewhere classified, initial encounter
Other complications of procedures, not elsewhere classified, initial encounter
Pressure ulcer of unspecified site, unspecified stage
Encounter for screening for cardiovascular disorders
Presence of coronary angioplasty implant and graft
Presence of other cardiac and vascular implants and grafts
Stenosis of coronary artery stent, initial encounter
Stenosis of cardiac vascular graft, initial encounter
Other specified complication of cardiac prosthetic devices, implants and grafts, initial encounter
Presence of other cardiac implants and grafts
Presence of fully implantable artificial heart
Presence of other specified functional implants
Breakdown (mechanical) of heart valve prosthesis, initial encounter
Other mechanical complication of heart valve prosthesis, initial encounter
Infection and inflammatory reaction due to cardiac valve prosthesis, initial encounter
Peripheral vascular angioplasty status with implants and grafts
Presence of other vascular implants and grafts
Breakdown (mechanical) of other vascular grafts, initial encounter
Other mechanical complication of other vascular grafts, initial encounter
Breakdown (mechanical) of cardiac electrode, initial encounter
Breakdown (mechanical) of cardiac pulse generator (battery), initial encounter
Displacement of cardiac electrode, initial encounter
Other mechanical complication of cardiac electrode, initial encounter
Infection and inflammatory reaction due to other cardiac and vascular devices, implants and grafts, initial encounter

billingRules.name: PTCA + Stent Same Vessel
billingRules.description: Cannot bill PTCA (92920) and stent placement (92928) on the same vessel
billingRules.learnMoreContent: When a stent is placed, the angioplasty is bundled into the stent code. Report only 92928 for the vessel where a stent was deployed.

billingRules.name: PTCA + Atherectomy Same Vessel
billingRules.description: Cannot bill PTCA (92920) and atherectomy (92924) on the same vessel
billingRules.learnMoreContent: When atherectomy is performed, the angioplasty is bundled. Report only 92924 for the vessel where atherectomy was performed.

billingRules.name: Atherectomy + Stent Same Vessel
billingRules.description: Use 92933 instead of 92924 + 92928 on the same vessel
billingRules.learnMoreContent: When both atherectomy and stent are performed on the same vessel, use the combined code 92933 (Atherectomy + Stent).

billingRules.name: CTO Codes Mutually Exclusive
billingRules.description: 92943 (CTO antegrade) and 92945 (CTO combined) are mutually exclusive
billingRules.learnMoreContent: Only one CTO code can be billed per session. Use 92943 for antegrade-only approach, 92945 for combined antegrade/retrograde.

billingRules.name: IVL Requires Base PCI
billingRules.description: 92972 (Coronary lithotripsy) requires a base PCI code
billingRules.learnMoreContent: 92972 is an add-on code that must be reported with a base PCI procedure code.

billingRules.name: Thrombectomy Requires Base PCI
billingRules.description: 92973 (Mechanical thrombectomy) requires a base PCI code
billingRules.learnMoreContent: 92973 is an add-on code that must be reported with a base PCI procedure code.

billingRules.name: Additional IVUS Requires Initial
billingRules.learnMoreContent: The additional vessel IVUS code (92979) can only be billed when the initial vessel IVUS (92978) is also performed.

billingRules.name: Additional FFR Requires Initial
billingRules.learnMoreContent: The additional vessel FFR code (93572) can only be billed when the initial vessel FFR (93571) is also performed.

billingRules.name: DCB Add-on Requires Base PCI
billingRules.description: 0914T (DCB add-on) requires a base PCI code
billingRules.learnMoreContent: 0914T is an add-on code for drug-coated balloon treatment on a separate target lesion from the primary PCI procedure.

billingRules.name: IVUS Bundled with DCB
billingRules.description: IVUS/OCT is bundled in DCB codes and should not be billed separately
billingRules.learnMoreContent: Drug-coated balloon codes (0913T, 0914T) include IVUS/OCT imaging. Do not bill imaging codes separately.

billingRules.name: Missing Vessel Modifier
billingRules.description: PCI codes require vessel/modifier selection for Medicare billing
billingRules.learnMoreContent: All PCI base codes require a vessel modifier (LC, LD, RC, LM, RI) to identify which coronary artery was treated.

billingRules.name: Right Heart Cath Overlap
billingRules.description: 93451 (R heart cath) cannot be billed with codes that include R heart cath
billingRules.learnMoreContent: 93451 is a standalone right heart catheterization code. It cannot be billed with combined codes that already include right heart catheterization.

billingRules.name: Left Heart Cath Overlap
billingRules.description: 93452 (L heart cath) cannot be billed with codes that include L heart cath
billingRules.learnMoreContent: 93452 is a standalone left heart catheterization code. It cannot be billed with combined codes that already include left heart catheterization.

billingRules.name: Combined Heart Cath Overlap
billingRules.description: 93453 (combined L+R) cannot be billed with standalone L or R heart cath codes
billingRules.learnMoreContent: 93453 includes both left and right heart catheterization. Do not bill standalone codes separately.

billingRules.name: Coronary Angiography Codes Mutually Exclusive
billingRules.description: Only one coronary angiography code (93454-93461) can be billed per session
billingRules.learnMoreContent: Coronary angiography codes 93454-93461 are mutually exclusive. Select the single code that best represents all components performed.

billingRules.name: Coronary Angio with Standalone Cath
billingRules.description: Cannot bill standalone heart cath codes with coronary angio codes that include the same component
billingRules.learnMoreContent: Coronary angiography codes 93455-93461 include heart catheterization components. Do not bill standalone cath codes for the same components.

billingRules.name: Diagnostic Cath + PCI Modifier
billingRules.description: Diagnostic cath with PCI same day may need modifier -59
billingRules.learnMoreContent: When diagnostic catheterization is performed with PCI on the same day, modifier -59 should be added to the diagnostic cath code if the criteria for separate billing are met.

billingRules.name: Duplicate PCI Same Vessel
billingRules.description: Multiple PCI codes assigned to the same vessel
billingRules.learnMoreContent: Generally, only one PCI code should be billed per major coronary artery. Multiple codes may be appropriate for separate lesions in different segments.

cciEdits.description: Left heart cath bundles coronary angiography supervision
Combined heart cath bundles coronary angiography supervision
Combined heart cath bundles right heart cath imaging
Combined heart cath bundles right heart cath with angiography
Combined heart cath bundles right heart cath
Combined heart cath bundles left heart cath
PCI with stent bundles PTCA
PCI with stent + atherectomy bundles PTCA
PCI with stent in bypass graft bundles PTCA
PCI with stent bundles PTCA with atherectomy (different vessel allowed)
Acute MI PCI bundles PTCA
Acute MI PCI bundles standard PCI with stent (different vessel)
Left heart cath with coronary angiography bundles left heart cath alone
Cath with injection bundles left heart cath
Cath with injection bundles combined heart cath
Cath with injection (R+L) bundles left heart cath
Cath with injection (R+L) bundles combined heart cath
Complete TTE with Doppler bundles separate Doppler echo
Complete TTE with Doppler bundles Doppler follow-up
Complete TTE with Doppler bundles Doppler color flow
TEE bundles separate Doppler echo
TEE bundles Doppler color flow
TEE with 3D bundles standard TEE
Stress echo with Doppler bundles stress echo without Doppler
Stress echo with Doppler bundles separate Doppler
Stress echo with Doppler bundles Doppler color flow
Initial TTE bundles follow-up TTE
Complete TTE Doppler bundles follow-up TTE
Comprehensive EP study bundles bundle of His recording
Comprehensive EP study bundles intra-atrial recording
Comprehensive EP study bundles right ventricular recording
Comprehensive EP study bundles intra-atrial pacing
Comprehensive EP study bundles intraventricular pacing
SVT ablation bundles comprehensive EP study
VT ablation bundles comprehensive EP study
AFib ablation bundles comprehensive EP study
AFib ablation bundles LA pacing and recording
AFib ablation bundles LV pacing and recording
SVT ablation bundles bundle of His recording
VT ablation bundles bundle of His recording
Critical care bundles subsequent hospital care (separate service with -25)
Critical care bundles subsequent hospital care (separate service with -25)
Critical care bundles subsequent hospital care (separate service with -25)
Critical care bundles left heart cath (separate service)
Critical care bundles combined heart cath (separate service)
Critical care bundles PCI with stent (separate service)
Critical care bundles IABP insertion (separate service)
Critical care bundles Impella insertion (separate service)
Fem/pop stent + atherectomy bundles iliac stent (different territory)
Fem/pop stent + atherectomy bundles iliac stent
Tibial stent bundles fem/pop stent (different territory)
Third order selective catheterization bundles first order (same vessel)
Third order selective catheterization bundles second order (same vessel)
Additional second+ order catheterization bundles second order (same vessel)

modifiers.name: Significant, Separately Identifiable E/M
modifiers.description: E/M service performed on the same day as a procedure or other service, significant and separately identifiable

modifiers.name: Professional Component
modifiers.description: Professional component only (reading/interpretation) — physician billing for interpretation without technical component

modifiers.name: Technical Component
modifiers.description: Technical component only — facility billing for equipment and technician, no professional interpretation

modifiers.name: Distinct Procedural Service
modifiers.description: Distinct procedural service performed on the same day — used for NCCI unbundling when procedures are truly separate

modifiers.name: Separate Structure
modifiers.description: Separate structure — services performed on different anatomical structures (e.g., left vs right heart cath)

modifiers.name: Separate Encounter
modifiers.description: Separate encounter — services performed during different encounters on the same date (e.g., morning echo + afternoon stress echo)

modifiers.name: Unusual Non-Overlapping Service
modifiers.description: Unusual non-overlapping service — services that do not overlap but are not typically reported together

modifiers.name: Repeat Procedure, Same Physician
modifiers.description: Repeat procedure by the same physician on the same day — must document medical necessity for repeat

modifiers.name: Repeat Procedure, Different Physician
modifiers.description: Repeat procedure by a different physician on the same day

modifiers.name: Bilateral Procedure
modifiers.description: Bilateral procedure — same procedure performed on both sides (e.g., bilateral lower extremity angiography)

modifiers.name: Left Side
modifiers.description: Procedure performed on the left side

modifiers.name: Right Side
modifiers.description: Procedure performed on the right side

modifiers.name: Increased Procedural Service
modifiers.description: Increased procedural service — work required substantially exceeded usual. Requires operative report documentation.

modifiers.name: Multiple Procedures
modifiers.description: Multiple procedures performed during same session — payment reduction applies to secondary/subsequent procedures

modifiers.name: Reduced Services
modifiers.description: Procedure partially completed or reduced in scope — requires documentation of what was performed and why it was reduced

modifiers.name: Discontinued Procedure
modifiers.description: Procedure started but discontinued due to threat to patient wellbeing — requires documentation of reason for discontinuation

modifiers.name: Decision for Surgery
modifiers.description: E/M service that resulted in the initial decision to perform a major procedure (90-day global). Use instead of -25 when the E/M led to the surgical decision.

modifiers.name: Unrelated E/M During Postop
modifiers.description: E/M service performed during a postoperative global period for a reason unrelated to the original procedure

modifiers.name: Unplanned Return to OR/Cath Lab
modifiers.description: Unplanned return to the operating room or cath lab during the postoperative period for a procedure related to the original

modifiers.name: Unrelated Procedure During Postop
modifiers.description: Procedure performed during the postoperative period that is unrelated to the original procedure

modifiers.name: Two Surgeons
modifiers.description: Two surgeons performing distinct parts of the same procedure — each surgeon bills with -62. Requires operative notes from both.

modifiers.name: Assistant Surgeon
modifiers.description: Surgical assistant — bills the same procedure code as the primary surgeon with -80. Reimbursed at 16% of full fee.
